content:
# Static Corrections

**Summary**: Corrections applied to seismic data to account for variations in the near-surface velocity.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2011-toma|Toma, 2011]] evaluated static-corrections using both single-layer and multi-layer models.

- [[2021-almashhor|AlMashhor, 2021]] discussed the importance of accurate first arrival picking for effective static corrections.

- [[2022-aljishi|AlJishi, 2022]] addressed static-corrections in sand dune environments where high-frequency delays are common.

- [[2023-muhammad|Muhammad, 2023]] evaluated the impact of static-corrections on velocity estimation accuracy.

- [[2002-al-ali|Al-Ali, 2002]] used the derived velocity models to calculate static corrections for seismic reflection data.

## Scientific Context

- [[2011-toma|Toma, 2011]] Critical for aligning pre-stack traces before NMO application.

- [[2021-almashhor|AlMashhor, 2021]] discussed the importance of accurate first arrival picking for effective static corrections.

- [[2022-aljishi|AlJishi, 2022]] addressed static-corrections in sand dune environments where high-frequency delays are common.

- [[2023-muhammad|Muhammad, 2023]] evaluated the impact of static-corrections on velocity estimation accuracy.

- [[2002-al-ali|Al-Ali, 2002]] used the derived velocity models to calculate static corrections for seismic reflection data.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2011-toma|Toma, 2011]] Comparison of high and low frequency components.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- The difference between multi-layer and single-layer low frequency statics ranged from -11 to +120 ms. ([[2011-toma|Toma, 2011, p. 43]])


- Static correction accounts for elevation differences and lateral variations in velocity/thickness of the weathering layer. ([[2021-almashhor|AlMashhor, 2021, p. 17]])

- A two-step solution is required: first removing high-frequency delays from dunes, then medium-wavelength issues from the underlying weathered layer. ([[2022-aljishi|AlJishi, 2022, p. 46]])

- Statics can lead to a 35% error in estimated velocity if they are not correctly accounted for. ([[2023-muhammad|Muhammad, 2023, p. 18]])

- Accurate knowledge of velocity from surface to a reference datum is essential for accurate statics. ([[2002-al-ali|Al-Ali, 2002, p. 108]])
